description: Evidentia-original baseline DD questionnaire. ~20 questions across FFIEC vendor-management domains. Apache 2.0 / no third-party licensing constraints. Use when no specific industry framework is mandated.

questions.question_text: Provide a copy of your most recent Information Security Policy or attestation that one is in place + reviewed within the last 12 months.
questions.notes: Acceptable evidence: policy PDF; auditor attestation letter; SOC 2 Type II Section IV control reference.

questions.question_text: Identify your Chief Information Security Officer (or equivalent role) and confirm whether the role reports directly to executive leadership.
Has your organization undergone an independent third-party security audit (SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, FedRAMP, or equivalent) in the last 12 months?
questions.notes: If Yes, provide the report or attestation letter and indicate the audit period covered.

questions.question_text: Is multi-factor authentication enforced for all administrative + privileged accounts that access systems handling our data?
Describe your access-review cadence for privileged accounts (frequency + reviewer + revocation SLA).
Are role-based access controls (RBAC) or attribute-based access controls (ABAC) enforced on systems handling customer data?

What categories of our data does your organization process, store, or transmit? (Personal Identifiable Information / Payment Card Industry / Protected Health Information / Confidential Business / Other)
Is data encrypted at rest using AES-256 (or stronger) and in transit using TLS 1.2 (or stronger)?
Describe your data retention + secure-disposal policy. State the maximum retention period for our data + the disposal method (cryptographic erasure / NIST 800-88 sanitization / physical destruction).
In which geographic regions is our data stored or processed? Provide region identifiers (e.g., AWS us-east-1, Azure North Europe) for each.
questions.notes: Drives regulator-facing data-residency analysis. Critical for cross-border-transfer compliance.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a documented Incident Response Plan and test it at least annually?
What is your contractual notification SLA for security incidents materially affecting our data? (Provide hours or business days.)
questions.notes: Many regulated industries require ≤72-hour notification (GDPR Art. 33 + variants).

questions.question_text: Have you experienced a security incident in the last 24 months that required notification to customers or regulators? If yes, summarize remediation + lessons learned.
questions.notes: Recent-incident disclosure; not disqualifying — operators want to see organizational learning.

questions.question_text: What is your Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) for the systems handling our data?
Do you maintain a documented Business Continuity Plan + Disaster Recovery Plan, and how often are they exercised?
List all subcontractors / sub-processors / cloud providers that have access to or process our data, with their service category + geographic location.
questions.notes: Required for FFIEC + OCC 2013-29 vendor-of-vendor concentration-risk analysis. Operator will reconcile with their internal disclosure expectations.

questions.question_text: Do you flow down equivalent data-protection contractual terms to your sub-processors that we have with you?
response_options: Yes — flow-down clauses in standard contract

questions.question_text: Do all employees + contractors with access to our data undergo background checks before onboarding + receive annual security awareness training?
What is the coverage limit of your cyber-liability insurance policy + does the policy cover incidents affecting customer data?
questions.notes: Provide policy summary or attestation; full policy text NOT required.

questions.question_text: Identify all regulatory + industry frameworks your organization complies with that are relevant to the services you provide us (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR, FFIEC, NYDFS 500).
